response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency systems, and developer tooling, but has no demonstrated experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU or AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or deep-learning workload optimization — all of which are core requirements for this Principal Performance Architect role. His work at Dunnhumby on high-throughput, low-latency services and his recent AI-agent tooling contributions show solid engineering fundamentals, but the gap between his profile and the hardware/AI accelerator domain is too wide to bridge at the principal IC level.
strengths: ~14 years of engineering experience across embedded, backend, and systems software, formally satisfying the BS + 8yr education/experience threshold
Demonstrated low-latency and high-throughput performance work (async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, caching in DLang at Dunnhumby handling millions of RPS)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing some alignment with the AI domain
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or any chip/accelerator design methodology — the primary technical domain of this role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD architectures, which are all preferred or required qualifications

Entire career has been application-layer and platform engineering (web, mobile, backend); no evidence of engagement with hardware performance analysis or system architecture at the silicon or firmware level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No deep learning or GPU workload expertise
